title: Hallucinations denied but behavior suggests internal preoccupation
note:
Date / Interval Update: No specific date or interval update documented in the source.

Symptom Review: Patient denies auditory or visual hallucinations this morning. During the interview, the patient pauses for long stretches and appears to look toward the corner of the room before answering. Nursing overnight notes report the patient was observed laughing to self and appearing internally preoccupied.

Medications / Changes / Side Effects: Patient is taking haloperidol as ordered, confirmed by medication administration record.

Mental Status / Observations: Patient denies hearing voices, stating "No, I'm not hearing voices" and "I'm just thinking." Nursing notes describe the patient laughing to self, staring intermittently, and appearing internally preoccupied.

Insight / Judgment: Not explicitly documented in the source.

Safety / Risk: No explicit safety or risk statements documented.

Assessment: Patient denies hallucinations; however, nursing observations overnight indicate laughing to self and internal preoccupation. Patient's behavior during interview includes prolonged pauses and looking toward the corner of the room. Source conflict remains unresolved in the provided material.
